---
title: "Cambridge University Press Quarto Template"
running-title: "CUP-Quarto"
format:
  cambridge-pdf:
    keep-tex: true
cambridge_journal: medium
manuscript_type: article
year: 2026
volume: 1
doi: "10.1017/example.2026.001"
ack-show: true
contrib-show: true
financial-show: true
conflicts-show: true
ethics-show: true
dates-show: true
copyright-show: true
received: "1 January 2026"
revised: "1 March 2026"
accepted: "1 April 2026"
author:
  - name: Florence Author
    corresponding: true
    email: florence.author@cambridge.ac.uk
    orcid: 0000-0001-2345-6789
    affiliations:
      - name: Department of Statistics, University of Cambridge
        city: Cambridge
        postal-code: CB3 0WB
        country: United Kingdom
  - name: Samuel Author
    orcid: 0000-0002-3456-7890
    affiliations:
      - name: School of Mathematics, University of Edinburgh
        city: Edinburgh
        postal-code: EH9 3FD
        country: United Kingdom
  - name: Tatiana Author
    affiliations:
      - name: School of Mathematics, University of Edinburgh
        city: Edinburgh
        postal-code: EH9 3FD
        country: United Kingdom
keywords: [template, demo, cambridge]
acknowledgments: |
  We are grateful for the technical assistance of A. Author.
fundingstatement: |
  This research was supported by grants from the Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC).
competing: |
  The authors declare no competing interests.
dataavailability: |
  Replication data and code are available at <https://example.org/data>.
ethicalstandards: |
  The research meets all ethical guidelines, including adherence to the legal requirements of the study country.
authorcontributions: |
  Conceptualization: F.A.; S.A. Methodology: F.A.; S.A. Data curation: T.A. Writing original draft: F.A.; S.A. All authors approved the final submitted draft.
abstract: |
  This document is a template demonstrating the Cambridge University Press journal format adapted for Quarto. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat.
bibliography: example.bib
---

# Introduction {#sec-intro}

This demo file is intended to serve as a "starter file" for preparing manuscript submissions to Cambridge University Press journals using Quarto.

By default, this template uses `biblatex` and adopts the Chicago referencing style. However, the journal you're submitting to may require a different reference style; specify the journal you're using with the `cambridge_journal` option.

@Bayer_etal_2013 reports findings consistent with @Adade_etal_2007. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat.

## Subsection example

Subsection text here. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

### Subsubsection example

Subsubsection text here. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

# Equations

Equations work as expected, both inline $f(x) = i \times n + 1$ and as block equations:

$$
f(x) = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i
$$ {#eq-mean}

You can refer to @eq-mean from the text. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

# Figures and Tables

```{r}
#| label: fig-test
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 3.5
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| fig-cap: "A simple test figure produced by ggplot2."
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw()
```

I can refer to @fig-test from the text.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-test
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| tbl-cap: "A short summary of the `mtcars` data."
knitr::kable(head(mtcars))
```

Tables can be cross-referenced as well, see @tbl-test.

# Conclusion

The conclusion text goes here. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.
